title: Dukkha Unsatisfactoriness
definition: A central concept in Buddhism, referring to the inherent suffering and dissatisfaction caused by desire, craving, ignorance, and clinging to temporary states.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A central concept in Buddhism, referring to the inherent suffering and dissatisfaction caused by desire, craving, ignorance, and clinging to temporary states.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A central concept in Buddhism, referring to the inherent suffering and dissatisfaction caused by desire, craving, ignorance, and clinging to temporary states.
difficulty_levels.expert: The recognition that trying to hold onto any peak state is futile, as everything is transient and impermanent, leading to a constant need-to-water (suffering).

extra_fields.insight: The recognition that trying to hold onto any peak state is futile, as everything is transient and impermanent, leading to a constant need-to-water (suffering).
